description: The village: homes gathered into community. 'sila' (community) plus the 'wo' of 'womu' (home): the word assembles the settlement the way the settlement assembled itself. Smaller than a city wants to be, larger than one household can be.
sound_symbolism: 'sila' opens it as the people, and 'wo' roofs them. The word ends on the home-syllable because that is what a village is made of.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'mia mua silawo menui.' (I dwell in the village.) 'lo miona silawo lorima.' (The people build a village.)

pillars.solarpunk-values: Human-scale settlement is a design target again: walkable, food-adjacent, sized so that neighbors are neighbors.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: For most of human history, this was the size a life came in: everyone known, everything walkable, the fields in sight of the roofs.
